title: 10 best herbal creams for eye bags

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the area.
Hydrating: The moisturizing and humectant properties of aloe vera help lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and fine lines.

Antioxidant-rich: Aloe vera's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help protect the delicate skin under the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature aging and discoloration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help reduce puffiness and dark circles. The cream cools and calms the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags. It also helps to improve circulation, which can help reduce the buildup of fluids that cause puffiness, leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with eye bags by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Chrysophanic acid: Chrysophanic acid, a diterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the appearance of eye bags by soothing and calming the skin around the eyes.

Vitamins (A, C, and E): Vitamins A, C, and E are antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the formation of eye bags, and also promote collagen production to improve skin elasticity and firmness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in aloe vera gel, which helps to reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes.
Leaves: A source of aloin, a compound that improves skin elasticity, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Leaves: Contain glycoproteins and other nutrients that help to soothe and hydrate the skin under the eyes, reducing the visibility of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Grate 1 cup of aloe vera gel from the leaf of aloe barbadensis.
Melt 2 tablespoons of cocoa butter in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Whisk in 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil until smooth.
Combine the aloe vera gel with the melted mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate for 30 minutes to set.

remedy_properties: Caffeine: Constricts blood vessels, reducing swelling and darkness under the eyes.
Tannins: Acts as an astringent, shrinking the appearance of puffiness and tightening the skin around the eyes.
Antioxidants (such as polyphenols): Neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to eye bag formation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with eye bags because of its high antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves contain catechins, which have anti-inflammatory effects and help to reduce puffiness. Applying Camellia sinensis creams to the under-eye area can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags. The cream's moisturizing properties also hydrate the skin, leaving it feeling smooth and refreshed, while its anti-aging properties can help to reduce fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, specifically epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help reduce eye bags by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and antioxidants, which in turn reduce puffiness and swelling.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate eye bag discomfort by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, which can help reduce puffiness and dark circles.

Gallocatechin: Gallocatechin, a type of catechin, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce eye bag appearance by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants, particularly theaflavins, which help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers are high in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discoloration.
Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are rich in catechins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 teaspoons of camellia sinensis leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Combine 1/4 cup of the camellia sinensis infusion with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 120 to 140 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the double boiler from the heat, let the mixture cool, and whip it until it thickens into a cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes by reducing inflammation.
Antioxidant properties: Herbal ginger creams contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help neutralize free radicals that can cause skin damage and dark circles under the eyes.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginger has a vasodilatory effect, which means it helps dilate blood vessels, improving blood circulation and reducing fluid retention in the eyes, thus reducing eye bags and puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with eye bags because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce puffiness and swelling. The antioxidants present in ginger creams combat free radicals that can cause dark circles and discoloration. Additionally, the natural cooling properties of ginger help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, providing a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance. This makes ginger creams a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution to minimize the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eye area by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby soothing and calming the skin.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing eye bag appearance by minimizing fluid retention and swelling in the delicate skin around the eyes.
Zingerone: This constituent helps to increase blood circulation and reduce fluid retention in the eye area, thereby minimizing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part, as they contain high concentrations of gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation.
Roots: Roots are also used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
Buds: Buds are used for their ability to reduce puffiness and swelling, as they contain compounds that help to constrict blood vessels and improve lymphatic drainage.
remedy_recipe: Extract the fresh rhizomes of the plant, cutting them into small pieces weighing approximately 50 grams.
Combine the rhizome pieces with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler, heating it slowly.
Allow the mixture to simmer for about 2 hours, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2:1, melting the mixture in a double boiler.
Pour the melted mixture into small containers, allowing it to cool and solidify completely in about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and puffiness under the eyes, combating signs of fatigue and stress.

Cooling and soothing effects: The menthol in peppermint cream provides a cooling sensation that constricts blood vessels, reducing dark circles and puffiness, and leaving the area feeling calm and refreshed.
Decongestant properties: Peppermint's natural decongestant properties help to reduce swelling and fluid retention under the eyes, making it an effective remedy for puffy or swollen eye bags.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with eye bags because of its cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint cream can help constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. Additionally, peppermint's refreshing and soothing effects can help to reduce stress and fatigue, common causes of eye bags. By applying peppermint cream to the under-eye area, you can experience a cooling sensation and a reduction in eye bag appearance, leaving your eyes looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and puffiness associated with eye bags.
Menthol: As a terpene, menthol has vasoconstrictive properties, which can help reduce blood flow to the affected area and alleviate the appearance of eye bags.
Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oils from Mentha x piperita leaves are commonly used in creams for eye bags due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Flowers: Mentha x piperita flowers are used in creams for eye bags because of their antioxidant and calming effects, which help reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Roots: Mentha x piperita roots are used in creams for eye bags due to their ability to improve circulation and reduce swelling, making them a popular ingredient in skincare products.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a warm place with air circulation for 7-10 days.
Infuse 5 grams of dried mentha x piperita in 100ml of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Mix 20-30 grams of beeswax and 10-15 grams of shea butter in a double boiler and melt at 180-200 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the infused oil with the melted wax mixture and stir until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags, soothing and calming the delicate skin around the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in eyebright cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles and discoloration under the eyes.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Eyebright's vasoconstrictive agents help constrict blood vessels, which can become dilated due to fluid retention or other factors, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, creams helps with eye bags because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb's ability to reduce puffiness and swelling makes it an effective remedy for dark circles and eye bags. Its rich nutrient profile, including vitamins A and C, also helps to nourish and rejuvenate the delicate skin around the eyes. This natural and gentle approach can help to reduce the appearance of eye bags, leaving the skin looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have antioxidant properties that can help reduce dark circles and discoloration under the eyes.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote blood vessel health, both of which can contribute to eye bags.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Euphrasia officinalis are used to make creams for eye bags because they contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce puffiness and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Euphrasia officinalis are used to make creams for eye bags because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
Roots: The roots of Euphrasia officinalis are used to make creams for eye bags because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce eye swelling and improve skin elasticity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested material at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve its medicinal properties completely.
Grind 10g of dried euphrasia officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle slowly.
Mix 5g of the powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a heat-proof container gently.
Stir the mixture at 60°c for 10 minutes and then let it cool before whipping into a cream thoroughly.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, the active compound found in pot marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, alleviating the appearance of eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can contribute to eye bag formation.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Pot marigold's vasoconstrictive effects help reduce blood flow to the area under the eyes, which can minimize the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's soothing action reduces puffiness and swelling, while its ability to promote collagen production helps to firm and smooth out the skin. Additionally, calendula's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections and promote healthy skin cell growth, leading to a reduction in the appearance of eye bags and a more refreshed and revitalized appearance. This makes calendula creams an effective natural remedy for tackling eye bags.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce puffiness and inflammation in eye bags by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the breakdown of excess fluids.

Flavonoids: Specifically, flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of eye bags.

Nascent Oils (especially Linoleic acid): These essential fatty acids, particularly linoleic acid, help to regenerate and nourish the skin, reducing the visibility of eye bags by hydrating and plumping up the skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most used part in creams for eye bags due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
Seeds: They are used due to their high content of essential fatty acids, which can help nourish and moisturize the skin around the eyes.
Leaves: They are utilized for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce water retention and prevent infections in the eye area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh calendula flowers on a sunny day and dry them for 2 to 3 weeks.
Steep 1 ounce of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Combine 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler and stir until the beeswax melts and the cream thickens.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes by minimizing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chamomile, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Soften and calm skin: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects help relax facial muscles and reduce water retention under the eyes, resulting in a more even and radiant complexion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes. By calming and cooling the skin, chamomile cream helps to reduce the appearance of dark circles and eye bags, leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.

Matricaria ester (Matricaria ester is a compound made from the esterification of sesquiterpene alcohols): This compound helps to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help to reduce the appearance of eye bags by reducing swelling and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate eye bag swelling and discoloration.
Seeds: They are a good source of essential oils, including chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers from a pesticide-free area at dawn or in the early morning when they are dry.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine dried flowers with a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1 part flowers to 2 parts oil.
Steep the flower-oil mixture in a double boiler at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the herb's properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and mix it with a thickening agent like beeswax to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and swelling, which can contribute to the appearance of eye bags by soothing and calming the skin under the eyes.
Flavonoids: Act as powerful antioxidants, protecting the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage that can lead to puffiness and discoloration.
Licorice extract: Has anti-irritant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, while also soothing and calming the skin under the eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes and calms the skin, reducing puffiness and swelling. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles. By promoting healthy skin, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can leave the eyes looking refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin is a triterpenoid saponin that helps reduce eye bags by its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and swelling in the under-eye area.
Licoric acid: Licoric acid is a phenolic compound that acts as an antioxidant, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to eye bag formation.

18β-Glycyrrhetinic acid: 18β-Glycyrrhetinic acid is a triterpenoid that has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, helping to reduce puffiness and swelling in the under-eye area, and alleviate symptoms of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce puffiness and soothe the skin under the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to combat free radicals and reduce the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to tighten and firm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots, making sure they are free from contaminants and debris.
Crush 250g of the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powder with 500ml of distilled water and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax dissolves completely.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, and then whip the mixture into a creamy texture using a hand mixer for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Damask rose creams contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, alleviating the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich extracts in damask rose creams neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to delicate skin around the eyes, resulting in a brighter, more radiant complexion.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Damask rose creams contain compounds that promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin, making them effective for addressing the fine lines, creases, and discoloration associated with eye bags and dark circles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosa damascena, also known as damask rose, creams helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The essential oil extracted from the flowers of Rosa damascena has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the skin. When applied to the under-eye area, it reduces puffiness and dark circles by improving blood circulation and reducing fluid retention. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to hydrate and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving it looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Rosa Damascena Flavonoids: These flavonoids help reduce inflammation and swelling around the eyes, alleviating the appearance of eye bags.
Rosa Damascena Phenolic Acids: These phenolic acids have potent antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Rosa Damascena Terpenes: These terpenes exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the visibility of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their essential oils and antioxidants help to reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Seeds: They contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help to nourish and soften the skin around the eyes.
Buds: They contain a higher concentration of essential oils and antioxidants compared to mature flowers, making them a popular choice for eye creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of rosa damascena flowers in 200ml of carrier oil at 40c for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Weigh 30g of beeswax and 20g of emulsifying wax and melt them in a double boiler.
Gradually add 100ml of distilled water to the melted wax mixture and stir until it emulsifies.
Pour the infused oil into the emulsified wax mixture and stir until it thickens to a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asian pennywort contains antioxidants that help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and protecting the skin from environmental damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, reduce swelling and redness caused by eye bags, promoting a smoother and more even appearance around the delicate eye area.

Vasoconstricting properties: Asian pennywort's vasoconstricting agents help to constrict blood vessels under the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness by minimizing fluid accumulation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica, also known as asiatic pennywort, creams helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes. The cream also improves circulation, which can help to reduce the appearance of eye bags. Additionally, Centella asiatica creams have been shown to improve skin elasticity and firmness, giving the under-eye area a smoother and more youthful appearance. This makes it an effective natural remedy for reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_constituents: Asiatic Acid: This triterpene ester helps reduce inflammation and swelling under the eyes, which contributes to the appearance of eye bags.
Madecassic Acid: Another triterpene ester in Centella asiatica, madecassic acid helps improve skin elasticity and reduce puffiness by promoting collagen production and improving lymphatic drainage.
Madecasaponins: These saponin glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Centella asiatica are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles.
Roots: The roots of Centella asiatica are known for their adaptogenic properties, which help to reduce stress and promote overall skin health, contributing to a more youthful appearance.
Stems: The stems of Centella asiatica contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 24 hours to preserve its potency.
Steep 100g of dried centella asiatica in 1l of carrier oil such as coconut oil at 60°c for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of the herbal oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler until the mixture reaches 60°c.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut creams helps with eye bags because they contain a powerful ingredient called aesculin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes. The cream's ability to constrict blood vessels also helps to diminish dark circles and discoloration. Additionally, horse chestnut extracts have been shown to improve circulation and promote lymphatic drainage, further reducing the appearance of eye bags. This natural combination makes horse chestnut creams an effective solution for achieving a smoother, more radiant under-eye area.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin is a triterpene saponin that helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating puffiness and dark circles.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut extracts have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in horse chestnut creams have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eye tissue, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Horse chestnut roots contain a compound called escin, which helps to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the horse chestnut plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried aesculus hippocastanum root extract from a reputable supplier and set aside for further use.
Weigh out 200 milliliters of distilled water and heat it in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Add the extracted aesculus hippocastanum root to the heated water and stir gently for 10 minutes to allow for infusion.
Combine 30 grams of beeswax, 20 grams of coconut oil, and 50 milliliters of the infused aesculus hippocastanum solution in a mixing bowl and blend until smooth.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 60 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with eye bags because of its natural astringent properties that constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness and dark circles. The cream's anti-inflammatory ingredients also soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes, minimizing redness and swelling. Additionally, witch hazel's antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and promote healthy skin. By applying the cream gently under the eyes, you can effectively reduce the appearance of eye bags and achieve a more radiant and refreshed look.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Witch hazel's high concentration of tannins helps to reduce puffiness and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and tightening the skin, making it appear smoother and less swollen.
Astringent properties: The astringent nature of witch hazel helps to shrink pores and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, minimizing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.

Antioxidants: Witch hazel's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and discoloration around the eyes, promoting healthier-looking skin and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, which help reduce puffiness and inflammation under the eyes.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or by air drying for several days.
Combine 20% dried hamamelis virginiana leaf extract with 80% coconut oil and 10% beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to dissolve the wax and create a smooth consistency.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with eye bags because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce puffiness and swelling. The herbal ingredients, such as Echinacea and Calendula, work to calm and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing dark circles and discoloration. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the area, leaving the skin feeling smooth and refreshed. As a result, Kansas coneflower creams provide a natural and effective solution for reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and dark circles or discoloration under the eyes, further contributing to eye bags.
Vasoconstrictive effects: The cream's vasoconstrictive properties help reduce blood flow to the delicate skin under the eyes, reducing puffiness and inflammation, which are common causes of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Rhyzomes: High in sesquiterpenes, which have natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried echinacea angustifolia roots in sufficient quantity for the recipe and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine ground echinacea angustifolia root powder with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and steep for 30 minutes to create infusion liquid.
Filter the infusion liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container to remove solids and yield a clear herbal extract.
Mix 50g of beeswax pellets with 100ml of coconut oil and gently warm the mixture in a double boiler until fully melted and blended for 5 minutes.
Combine the filtered echinacea angustifolia extract with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture, then whip vigorously using an electric mixer to emulsify and aerate the cream for 3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with eye bags because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and dark circles. The cream's unique blend of natural ingredients, including Ceylon cinnamon, soothes and calms the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing swelling and discoloration. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich formula promotes collagen production, firming up the skin and giving it a smoother appearance. With regular use, eye bags begin to fade, leaving you with brighter, more radiant peepers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which helps to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes by combating free radicals and inflammation.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The active compounds in herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams help to constrict blood vessels, which reduces swelling and discoloration caused by eye bags, allowing for a more even tone and appearance.

Antimicrobial properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams contain antimicrobial agents that can help to prevent bacterial or fungal infections from causing puffiness, redness, or itchiness in the eyes, further reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce puffiness and soothe irritated skin around the eyes.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of bioactive compounds, rhyzomes provide natural astringent and antimicrobial properties to combat dark circles and eye fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality cinnamomum verum essential oil 2% concentration and other ingredients such as coconut oil and beeswax.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler and mix it with coconut oil until well combined at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Add the cinnamomum verum essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well for another 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into an air-tight container and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow it to set properly.
Once set, apply a small amount of the cream gently under your eyes using a circular motion morning and night as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with eye bags because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces puffiness and dark circles. The cream's active ingredients work to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, resulting in a brighter and more radiant appearance under the eyes. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to delicate skin tissue, leaving the area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, helps reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes by inhibiting prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause swelling.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to dark circles and puffy eyes, promoting overall skin health and reducing discoloration.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The bioactive compounds in turmeric, such as curcuminoids, help constrict blood vessels, which reduces the appearance of puffiness and dark circles by minimizing fluid accumulation under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Roots: Turmeric roots are known for their natural astringent and anti-irritant properties, making them effective in reducing eye bag discoloration and soothing the skin.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried turmeric roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground turmeric powder with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Stir in 10ml of coconut oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil until well combined for 2 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric whisk for 5 minutes.
Transfer the herbal cream to a sterilized glass jar, label, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks at 4°c.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with eye bags because of its unique properties that soothe and reduce puffiness. The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage work to constrict blood vessels, diminishing the appearance of dark circles and swelling under the eyes. Additionally, sage's astringent qualities help to tighten the skin, reducing the look of fine lines and wrinkles. As a result, herbal sage creams can leave the delicate eye area looking smoother, brighter, and more radiant, giving you a refreshed and revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sage creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor and borneol, which help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Sage contains antioxidants like rosmarinic acid, which help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in sage, such as salicylic acid, help constrict blood vessels and reduce excess fluid accumulation under the eyes, making it an effective ingredient for reducing puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Barks: Sage bark contains astringent properties that help constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine 20g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Steep the herb mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow for a gentle infusion process.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Whip the cooled herbal oil with 20g of beeswax and 2 drops of vitamin e oil until creamy and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with eye bags because of its unique ability to reduce puffiness and dark circles. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation under the eyes. Additionally, fennel's antioxidant properties work to combat free radicals that can cause discoloration and irritation. By applying a fennel cream under the eyes, you can say goodbye to unsightly bags and hello to brighter, more radiant-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel contains antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress around the eyes, alleviating puffiness and dark circles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce swelling and ease congestion under the eyes, making eye bags less noticeable.

Vasodilatory properties: The essential oils in fennel cream, such as anethole, can help to dilate blood vessels, which improves circulation and reduces fluid retention under the eyes, thus minimizing puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles.
Seeds: Containing anethole, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms the skin under the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare flowers from a trusted source and measure out 2 ounces for the recipe.
Combine the dried flowers with 4 ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the solids and discard the herbs.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then whip it with 1 ounce of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with eye bags because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and darkness. The herb's diuretic properties help to eliminate excess fluids and toxins, reducing the appearance of swelling under the eyes. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant-rich compounds combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, leading to dark circles and discoloration. As a result, dandelion creams provide a natural and gentle solution for reducing the appearance of eye bags, leaving you with brighter, more refreshed-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion cream can help reduce fluid retention, which may contribute to puffy under-eye bags by increasing urine production and reducing water accumulation in the body.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion cream may help soothe and calm inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and discoloration.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream contains a range of antioxidants that can protect the skin from environmental stressors, free radicals, and damage caused by pollution, which can contribute to the formation of dark circles and eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Fruits: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and chop them into small pieces.
Mix the chopped roots with 100 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 24 hours, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, resulting in an infused oil with 20% taraxacum officinale content.
Combine the infused oil with 200 grams of beeswax pellets and 50 milliliters of distilled water, heating the mixture to 65°c for 10 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye bags are topical treatments made from natural ingredients that help to reduce puffiness, dark circles, and discoloration under the eyes. They work by soothing and hydrating the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting circulation. The best herbal creams for eye bags contain ingredients such as Aloe barbadensis, Vitis vinifera, Camellia sinensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ruscus aculeatus, Cucumis sativus, Ginkgo biloba, Silybum marianum, Cinchona officinalis, and Panax ginseng. If left untreated, eye bags can make people appear tired, older, and less confident, negatively impacting their personal and professional lives.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye bags that are treated with herbal creams are dark circles, puffiness, and fine lines around the eyes. These herbal creams often contain ingredients like cucumber, aloe vera, green tea, and ginseng, which help reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and hydrate the skin. They may also include antioxidants and essential fatty acids to nourish and protect the delicate skin around the eyes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with eye bags is a blend of Cucumber and Aloe Vera creams. Cucumber cream reduces puffiness and cools the skin, while Aloe Vera cream hydrates and soothes the delicate skin around the eyes. Additionally, a small amount of Green Tea cream can be added to reduce inflammation and promote collagen production. This trio helps to reduce the appearance of eye bags, leaving the skin looking refreshed and revitalized.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with eye bags because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes. The cooling and soothing effects of the cream can also help to relax the skin and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Aloe vera help to combat free radicals that can cause premature aging and discoloration of the skin. This can result in a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_constituents: Chromones: Help reduce puffiness and inflammation associated with eye bags by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Anthraquinones: Assist in soothing and calming the skin, thereby minimizing the appearance of eye bags caused by dark circles and discoloration.
Flavonoids: Have a potent antioxidant effect that helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby alleviating the visible signs of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe barbadensis are primarily used due to their high content of aloe vera gel, which is rich in soothing and hydrating properties that can help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Gel from Leaves: The gel from Aloe barbadensis leaves is particularly used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote skin health around the eyes.

Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract from Aloe barbadensis leaves is often used in eye creams due to its ability to improve skin elasticity and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, which can contribute to the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and transfer it to a blender for emulsification.
Add 20 grams of distilled water and 10 grams of emulsifying wax to the blender for mixing.
Blend the mixture for 5 minutes at room temperature before adding 5 grams of beeswax.
Continue blending for another 5 minutes to achieve a smooth and consistent texture gradually.
Transfer the mixture to a glass jar and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera creams helps with eye bags because of their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The grape seed extract in Vitis vinifera creams has been shown to reduce puffiness and swelling by improving circulation and reducing fluid retention. This helps to tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags. Additionally, the antioxidants in Vitis vinifera creams protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote collagen production, resulting in smoother, more youthful-looking skin. Regular use can lead to a more rested and refreshed appearance.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce inflammation and improve circulation around the eyes, which can reduce the appearance of eye bags.
Oligomeric Proanthocyanidins (OPCs): Protect against free radicals and improve skin elasticity, which can help reduce puffiness and discoloration of eye bags.
Linoleic Acid: Nourish and hydrate the skin around the eyes, reducing the visibility of eye bags caused by dehydration and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Vitis vinifera fruits, also known as grapes, are commonly used due to their high content of antioxidants and vitamins that help reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are used in creams due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to tighten and soothe the skin around the eyes.

Seeds: Vitis vinifera seeds, particularly grape seed extract, are used in creams due to their high content of antioxidants and polyphenols, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh vitis vinifera leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for best results.
Grind 10g of harvested vitis vinifera material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground vitis vinifera powder with 50g of coconut oil and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to facilitate infusion and melting.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the herbal vitis vinifera cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis creams helps with eye bags because they contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, which work to reduce puffiness and inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of Camellia sinensis help to relax the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and bags. Additionally, the creams' ability to improve circulation and reduce fluid retention helps to minimize the appearance of eye bags, leaving the skin looking smoother and more refreshed. This makes Camellia sinensis creams a popular natural remedy for eye care.
remedy_constituents: Theaflavins: These flavonoids help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate eye bags caused by fluid retention and swelling.
Theogallin: This catechin derivative has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and discoloration associated with eye bags.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye bag severity by improving circulation and minimizing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are commonly used in skincare products due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes.
Flowers: Camellia sinensis flowers are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which contribute to their anti-aging and anti-inflammatory effects, making them suitable for eye care products.

Buds: The buds of Camellia sinensis are often used in skincare due to their high concentration of catechins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye bag appearance.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 2 cups of dried camellia sinensis and 1 cup of distilled water in a heatproof bowl.
Steep the camellia sinensis in boiling water for 5 minutes, then let it infuse for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until it reaches a temperature of 180°f in 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The licorice root extract, a key component in these creams, has been shown to reduce puffiness and dark circles by improving circulation and reducing fluid retention. The soothing and calming effects of Glycyrrhiza glabra also help to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles around the eyes, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to any skincare routine.
remedy_constituents: Liquiritin: Helps reduce inflammation and swelling under the eyes by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which in turn alleviate the appearance of eye bags.
Glycyrrhizin: Works as a diuretic to reduce water retention and decrease puffiness around the eyes, thereby minimizing the visibility of eye bags.
Flavonoids: Exhibits antioxidant properties to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress that contributes to dark circles and eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Leaves: They have a soothing and anti-inflammatory effect, which can help calm and reduce the appearance of eye bags.
Barks: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the skin around the eyes from damage and reduce the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in a clean mixing bowl.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered root with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 20 grams of distilled water and 10 grams of vitamin e oil then stir well.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with eye bags because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that reduce puffiness and swelling. The herb's ability to constrict blood vessels also minimizes the appearance of dark circles. Furthermore, Ruscus aculeatus creams contain antioxidants that combat free radicals, promoting a healthier and more even-toned complexion. As a result, the skin around the eyes appears smoother, firmer, and more radiant, effectively reducing the appearance of eye bags and making the area look more refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Astringent phenolics: Help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and minimizing fluid accumulation under the eyes.
Steroidal saponins: Assist in reducing inflammation and edema, which contribute to the appearance of eye bags.
Flavonoids: Act as potent antioxidants, protecting the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Stems: The stems may be used for similar purposes to the leaves, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
Roots: The roots are sometimes used for their potential anti-inflammatory properties to combat eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of ruscus aculeatus leaves and flowers in a clean basket for later use.
Dry the harvested ruscus aculeatus in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 100 grams of dried ruscus aculeatus with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain and filter the resulting liquid to obtain the herbal ruscus aculeatus cream base.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cucumis sativus creams helps with eye bags because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties derived from the cucumber plant. These properties help to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, while also soothing and hydrating the delicate skin. By cooling and calming the skin, cucumber creams can also help to reduce the appearance of dark circles and fine lines, leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective way to address the issue of eye bags.
remedy_constituents: Furocoumarins: These compounds help reduce the appearance of eye bags by inhibiting the breakdown of collagen and elastin, thereby improving skin elasticity and firmness.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress around the eyes, thereby reducing puffiness and dark circles.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as cucurbitacins, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and redness around the eyes, thereby reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Cucumis sativus are often used in skincare products due to their high content of antioxidants and essential fatty acids, which help to reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Fruits: The flesh of the cucumber fruit is rich in water and electrolytes, making it an effective ingredient in creams to reduce eye bags and hydrate the skin.
Leaves: Cucumber leaves contain a variety of nutrients and flavonoids that can help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them a popular ingredient in skincare products for eye care.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of cucumis sativus in 1l of carrier oil at 40c for 2 hours to create herbal extract.
Combine 100g of the infused herbal extract with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the bowl in a double boiler at 60c for 30 minutes to melt the ingredients and emulsify the mixture.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to 30c before whipping the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled and whipped mixture into a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with eye bags because it improves blood circulation and reduces puffiness. The antioxidants in Ginkgo biloba combat free radicals, which contribute to inflammation and dark circles under the eyes. Its anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles. Furthermore, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to tighten the skin, giving the eyes a more refreshed and rejuvenated look. Regular use can lead to a more radiant and youthful appearance.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce puffiness and dark circles by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes.
Bilobalide: Assist in reducing water retention and swelling by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that contributes to inflammation and fluid accumulation.

Ginkgolides: Show anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage and reduce the appearance of eye bags by promoting collagen production and improving skin elasticity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracts from the leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used in creams for eye bags due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the Ginkgo biloba tree are utilized to create creams for eye bags because they contain terpenoids, which help reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Leaves: The extracts from the leaves of Ginkgo biloba are also used in creams for eye bags due to their ability to improve circulation and reduce fluid retention.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 50 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves with 100 grams of jojoba oil in a mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes using a double boiler.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy using a hand mixer or electric whisk.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with eye bags because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and improves circulation around the delicate orbital area. By tackling puffiness and dark circles, these creams promote a more rested and refreshed appearance. Additionally, silymarin's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, allowing it to look smoother and more even-toned. Regular use of these creams can lead to a more radiant and youthful-looking complexion.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation under the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of eye bags.
Silymarin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce puffiness and discoloration associated with eye bags.
Polyphenols: Have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the delicate skin under the eyes from damage and reduce the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and silymarin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Flowers: They are used for their soothing and calming effects, which can help reduce eye strain and puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves from a clean environment by hand carefully.
Weigh and combine the silybum marianum with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Mix the silybum marianum and beeswax with 100 ml of sweet almond oil and 50 ml of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture to 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes using a thermometer to monitor temperature.
Stir the cooled mixture well and transfer it to a clean container to store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with eye bags because they contain quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces puffiness and swelling. The cream's soothing properties also calm and relax the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and discoloration. Additionally, the astringent properties of Cinchona officinalis help to tighten and firm the skin, providing a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance. Regular application can lead to a more radiant and youthful look.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It reduces inflammation and helps to alleviate puffiness by constricting blood vessels and improving lymphatic drainage.
Cinchonine: It possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Quinic acid: It has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Barks: Used for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties to tighten and soothe the skin under the eyes.
Roots: Not typically used for eye bag creams, however, the roots may be used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in other products.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried cinchona officinalis bark in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then cool it to room temperature in 30 minutes.
Melt 2 ounces of beeswax in a double boiler and let it cool slightly in 10 minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with 2 ounces of coconut oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a bowl.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and becomes a smooth consistency in 5-10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with eye bags because it contains adaptogenic properties that work to reduce puffiness and dark circles. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the delicate skin under the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags. Additionally, Panax ginseng is known to improve circulation, which can help to reduce swelling and promote a more even-toned complexion. By addressing the underlying causes of eye bags, Panax ginseng creams provide a natural and effective solution for a more radiant and refreshed appearance.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help reduce eye bags by improving circulation and lymphatic drainage, reducing puffiness and inflammation in the under-eye area.
Ginsenosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate eye bags by reducing swelling, redness, and irritation around the eyes.
Terpenoids: They possess antioxidant properties, which help combat oxidative stress and prevent damage to the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Panax ginseng due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for reducing eye bags.
Leaves: The leaves are used in some eye creams because they contain compounds that may help to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Barks: The barks are occasionally used in eye creams for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, although they may be less potent than the roots or leaves.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 grams of dried panax ginseng root and 2 tablespoons of beeswax for the base ingredients.
Combine 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of distilled water in a double boiler.
Add 2-3 grams of dried panax ginseng root to the coconut oil mixture and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture and combine it with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture for an additional 10-15 minutes to allow the beeswax to melt and mix.
